author: edgl_dev
content:
I have some questions/doubts about the EIP as i revised it today on ethrex, I might have some info wrong maybe though so take it with a grain of salt:

1.
Step 3 validates T against post-state S "by checking the nonce and balance of T.origin". It does not require T.max_fee_per_gas >= block.base_fee. A tx that fails that bound is invalid for inclusion under EIP-1559: no builder can put it in the block. With only nonce+balance, an omitted such tx is classified as "valid if appended" and yields INCLUSION_LIST_UNSATISFIED for a tx that can never be included, which makes the block unattestable for no reachable reason.

2. The balance threshold is not defined
"checking the balance" does not state the amount. Clients must agree on the exact figure or they will disagree on the verdict. We use gas_limit * max_fee_per_gas + value (blob cost excluded, blob txs are not IL-eligible). Maybe should state the formula in the EIP.

3.
The Specification's EL algorithm uses a single gas_left and the test T.gas > gas_left (step 2). Glamsterdam also includes EIP-8037, which meters regular-gas and state-gas separately and sets the header to gas_used = max(block_regular_gas_used, block_state_gas_used). There is no single "gas remaining" anymore, so gas_left is ambiguous.

4.
MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST = 8 KiB is given as a CL gossip/build bound (P2P rule 6 and IL Building). The EIP does not say whether the EL must reject an oversized or undecodable IL passed to newPayload / forkchoiceUpdated

author: jih2nn
content:
Thanks for asking!

1.
The EL checks if an IL transaction is [intrinsically valid](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/eips/bogota/eip-7805/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/fork.py#L1230) before the nonce and balance check.

2.
See [this](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/eips/bogota/eip-7805/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/fork.py#L1231) and [this](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/eips/bogota/eip-7805/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/fork.py#L634-L635).

While I'm not sure if the EIP should state as much as the spec does, but I agree that the EIP can be improved and a bit outdated. It's been on my list but I've been working on spec changes (and other stuff). I will prioritize the EIP task and start it tomorrow. Thank you for feedback!

3.
The spec was written way before EIP-8037 has included. I will rebase the spec accordingly. Thank you for pointing out!

4.
Engine APIs are trusted. The EL trusts the CL that it will consider ILs not exceeding 8 KiB limit. We also have a test vector checking if the EL produces an IL not exceeding the limit. Additionally, what the CL passes is a list of transactions, not ILs.

Let me know if anything is unclear or outdated!

author: nc1234
content: Anyone has any problem with this simplification? https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5324 I will proceed to fix up the tests if we want this simplification
